intro: Symphyobasis macroplectra, also known as Plectranthus, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, commonly found in the tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Symphyobasis macroplectra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to relieve symptoms of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and to promote wound healing.
